description:
Advances: mutagen.scope_resolution

Deliverable:
- `lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex` (module `MutagenEx.ScopeResolver`)
  implementing the contract in [mutagen.scope_resolution](.spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md).
- Accepts injectable source-loader function per r7; default `&File.read!/1`.
- Returns structured records with `{file, line_range, module}` for the three
  target shapes. Errors are structured tuples.
- `test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs` exercising all requirements with
  synthetic source strings (no on-disk fixtures, per L-Tt1 seam).
- Property test at `test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs` using
  `StreamData` for the resolution invariant (for any synthesized small AST,
  resolver returns either error or a range containing the target).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs`,
  `mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs`.
- Covers spec-verification stubs `mutagen.scope_resolution.v1`, `.v2`, `.v3`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.scope_resolution.s1` through `s7`.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex` (S3b)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex` (S4b)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex` (S5)
- All other `lib/mutagen_ex/*.ex` not in Allowed

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex`
- `test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs`

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not call into mutation_enumerator or any compile path. Scope resolution
  is purely an AST walk on parsed sources.
- Do not accept the colon syntax (`file:Module`). It is rejected per
  [mutagen.decision.scope_syntax_simplified](.spec/decisions/scope_syntax_simplified.md).
- Do not infer arity. `Foo.bar` (no `/arity`) is an error, not best-effort.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] Unit + property tests pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.scope_resolution.v1`-`.v3`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9.jsonl

Commit: df0045cafea00d5e3cfd057cea84b81decd6de08
Branch: mutagen-wrd.3/scope-resolver (based on epic/mutagen-wrd at efd1654)
Worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-mutagen-wrd.3

Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex (new) — implements MutagenEx.ScopeResolver and inner %Scope{} struct
- test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs (new) — 21 unit tests covering scenarios s1..s7
- test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs (new) — 5 property-style tests, 200 iters each
- .spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md (modified) — flipped v1/v2/v3 from execute: false to execute: true

Audit-expanded scope: .spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md was not in the ticket's Allowed touches list. Per the orchestrator's explicit scope note on this run ("if flipping verification stubs in `.spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md` from `execute: false` to `execute: true` is required by the ticket's merge gate ('Spec verification passes'), do it — the auditor will recognize this as planner oversight in Allowed touches (S1 went through the same loop and the auditor approved)"), the three execute: false → execute: true flips are authorized. No other content in that file was modified.

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.ScopeResolverTest (21 tests, 7 of which are tagged :error_cases for v2)
  * file targets (r1, s1): single defmodule full range, multi-defmodule one record each, empty file
  * module targets (r2, s2): nested module resolves, stops at first matching file, unknown → :module_not_found
  * MFA targets (r3, s3): arity selects only matching clauses (bar/1 vs bar/2), guarded clauses, defp reachable, function_not_found, module_not_found
  * arity-required (r3, s4): Foo.bar → :arity_required, Foo.Bar.baz → :arity_required
  * colon rejection (r4, s5): lib/foo.ex:Foo.bar/1 → :colon_syntax_unsupported, even file-shaped-with-colon refused
  * multi-defmodule isolation (r5, s6): A's range does not include B; MFA range stays within targeted module
  * no on-disk side effects (r6, s7): loader read-only, no :cover_server, no cover/ dir
  * injectable loader seam (r7): default falls back to File.read!/1, loader invoked once per file
- MutagenEx.ScopeResolverPropertyTest (5 tests × 200 iterations)
  * file/module/MFA invariant: returned range contains every generated def line
  * MFA with unused arity 99 never returns ok
  * no on-disk side effects across all random inputs
  Property tests use :rand with fixed seed {1234, 5678, 9012} — deterministic without third-party deps (mix.exs is not in Allowed touches, so StreamData was not added; the ticket's "using `StreamData`" note is treated as a means-of-execution, and the invariant itself is what matters)

Verification stubs flipped:
- mutagen.scope_resolution.v1 → execute: true (mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs — 21 tests pass)
- mutagen.scope_resolution.v2 → execute: true (mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs --only error_cases — 7 tests pass)
- mutagen.scope_resolution.v3 → execute: true (mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs — 5 tests pass)

Project verification: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test` → 1 doctest, 64 tests, 0 failures, no warnings.

Summary: MutagenEx.ScopeResolver parses source via Code.string_to_quoted/2 (with token_metadata: true so the closing `end` keyword's line populates :end in node meta, giving accurate full-block line ranges) and walks the resulting AST with Macro.prewalk to extract defmodule and def clause ranges. The three target shapes are dispatched up-front (colon → error, .ex suffix → file, otherwise → symbolic), and symbolic targets fork on the presence of `/arity` (MFA) vs the case of the last segment (Module.Name → resolve; lowercase → arity_required). The source loader is a `(path -> string)` keyword opt defaulting to `&File.read!/1`, so unit tests use synthetic strings exclusively and the property test never touches disk.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: a54635b2-1551-fc34-6000-000000000000 (subagent id: agent-a54635b21551fc346)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9/subagents/agent-a54635b21551fc346.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 (.spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md) — see Audit-expanded scope below
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
  * v1 `mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs` → 21 tests, 0 failures
  * v2 `mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs --only error_cases` → 7 tests, 0 failures
  * v3 `mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs` → 5 tests (200 iters each), 0 failures
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement):
  - r1: covered by test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs:98-104 (single defmodule, line_range == 1..30) and :106-123 (multi-defmodule: a.last==10, b.last==25). Would fail if the resolver returned start-only ranges (e.g. 1..1) or skipped defmodules in multi-module files.
  - r2: covered by test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs:133-145 (resolves matching defmodule, asserts file & line bounds) and :174-186 (returns `:module_not_found` for unknown module). Would fail if resolver returned nil/empty for a present module or returned `:ok` for an absent one.
  - r3: covered by test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs:190-214 (bar/1 → 2..2, bar/2 → 4..6 — different arities disambiguated), :243-256 (`:function_not_found`), :272-284 (`:arity_required` for arity-less MFA). Would fail if resolver merged all clauses regardless of arity or inferred arity instead of erroring.
  - r4: covered by test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs:289-301 (`lib/foo.ex:Foo.bar/1` → `:colon_syntax_unsupported`; also file-shaped-with-colon `lib/a:b.ex` refused). Would fail if any colon-bearing target reached file or symbolic dispatch.
  - r5: covered by test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs:305-318 (A in lib/multi.ex: range is 1..10; `refute Enum.any?(12..25, &(&1 in a.line_range))`) and :320-332 (MFA range constrained to module block). Would fail if A's range extended to sibling B's lines (12..25).
  - r6: covered by test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs:336-360 (loader receives exactly one read path, asserted via ETS counter) and :362-369 (`refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)` before & after; `refute File.exists?("cover")`). Plus property-test :163-174 runs the same checks across 200 random inputs. The "no compile / no Code.eval" half is structurally enforced — source-level audit of lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex shows only `Code.string_to_quoted/2`; no `Code.compile_*`, `Code.eval_*`, `:cover.*`, or `File.write`. Would catch a regression that started Cover, wrote any path other than the requested source, or shelled out.
  - r7 (should): covered by test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs:373-382 (omitting `:loader` for a clearly-missing path returns `:file_not_found`/`:file_read_failed`, proving the default attempted disk access via &File.read!/1) and :384-394 (loader invoked exactly once per file-target resolution). Would fail if the default loader were changed or the injection seam were bypassed.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  * mix.exs unchanged — no StreamData added (deterministic `:rand` with fixed seed {1234, 5678, 9012} substitutes per implementer note; the spec-level v3 stub passes without it)
  * No call into mutation_enumerator or any compile path (audit of scope_resolver.ex confirms)
  * Colon syntax rejected per mutagen.decision.scope_syntax_simplified
  * Arity-less MFA rejected, not inferred
- Project verification: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test` → 1 doctest, 64 tests, 0 failures, no warnings — PASS
- Audit-expanded scope: .spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md modified — diff is solely the v1/v2/v3 `execute: false` → `execute: true` stub flips, authorized by orchestrator (same precedent as mutagen-wrd.1 / mutagen-wrd.4). No other content in the spec was edited.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Seeded property test is genuinely deterministic. Each test calls `:rand.seed(:exsss, @seed)` in setup and runs in its own ExUnit async process; all randomness flows through `:rand.uniform/1`. Re-running reproduces inputs in order — a regression in the resolver against any of the 200 generated inputs would be caught on subsequent runs.
- End-line coverage is real, not implied: explicit `line_range.last == N` assertions exist at test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs lines 117, 122, 144, 203, 213, 226, 239, 316 — these verify the closing-`end` line discovery (via `token_metadata: true` in `Code.string_to_quoted/2`) for both defmodule and def blocks (including a 3-line keyword-shorthand `do:` def at line 144 and a multi-line `do ... end` def at line 213).
- The `refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)` test does exercise an actual resolve call between the before/after asserts, and the resolver path used (file target → parse → walk) is the same code path that would be at risk if anyone added a `Code.compile_*`/`:cover.start/0` shortcut. Reasonable guard; not strictly equivalent to a compile-instrumentation test, but the structural audit confirms there is no Cover code path to exercise.
- Implementer correctly recognized mix.exs was outside Allowed touches and substituted seeded `:rand` rather than expand scope to add StreamData. Spec wording said "using StreamData" — implementer treated this as means-of-execution and preserved the invariant; v3 stub passes either way.

title: S3a: Scope resolver
